text:
MEDICAL ONCOLOGY FOLLOW-UP NOTE
Department of Medical Oncology

ATTENDING PHYSICIAN: [Attending Oncologist]
PATIENT: Margaret Okonkwo
DOB: [on file]
MRN: [on file]
VISIT TYPE: Established patient follow-up — post-induction immunotherapy assessment / transition to maintenance

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT / REASON FOR VISIT:
Ms. Okonkwo returns today for her post-induction oncology assessment following completion of 4 cycles of ipilimumab/nivolumab induction. She is here to discuss transition to nivolumab maintenance, review of her overall clinical status and toxicity profile, and to plan restaging imaging.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Ms. Okonkwo is a 63-year-old woman with Stage IV clear cell renal cell carcinoma (ccRCC), right primary, with sarcomatoid differentiation (20%), bilateral pulmonary metastases, and regional retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy, diagnosed approximately 5 months ago. Her genomic profile is notable for biallelic VHL loss, PBRM1 frameshift, BAP1 loss (associated with sarcomatoid phenotype), SETD2 frameshift, TP53 R248W, and CDKN2A/B homozygous deletion — in aggregate a high-risk molecular profile consistent with her aggressive histology. Tumor mutational burden was low (5 mut/Mb), MSS. PD-L1 CPS 15-18 on both biopsy and surgical specimen.

She underwent right radical nephrectomy with retroperitoneal lymph node dissection approximately 4 months ago (R0 resection, pT3a pN1 M1, 5/8 nodes positive, extranodal extension in the largest node). She recovered uneventfully from surgery and initiated first-line ipi/nivo (ipilimumab 1 mg/kg + nivolumab 3 mg/kg IV q3 weeks x 4 cycles, per Checkmate 214 regimen) approximately 6 weeks post-nephrectomy.

She has now completed all 4 planned induction cycles. The treatment course was complicated by Grade 2 immune-related colitis after cycle 2, which developed approximately 5 weeks after initiation. She experienced 5-6 loose stools per day above baseline with lower abdominal cramping but no rectal bleeding. Infectious workup (C. diff, stool culture, O&P) was negative. She was managed with oral prednisone 1 mg/kg/day with gradual taper over 6 weeks; ipilimumab was held for one cycle during this time and nivolumab was continued. Diarrhea improved to Grade 1 within approximately 10 days of steroid initiation, and steroids were successfully tapered and discontinued. She completed cycles 3 and 4 of nivolumab (ipi was not re-introduced given colitis). Steroid taper was completed prior to today's visit.

Today, Ms. Okonkwo reports no current gastrointestinal symptoms. She denies new diarrhea, abdominal pain, or rectal bleeding. She notes mild fatigue, which she attributes partly to the ongoing cancer treatment and possibly to the steroid taper. She denies new respiratory symptoms — no cough, dyspnea, hemoptysis, or chest pain. She has no fevers, night sweats, or significant weight change beyond the approximately 2-3 lb loss she noticed over the prior month. She denies arthralgias, rash, or visual changes. Appetite is fair to good. She is maintaining her activities at home without difficulty.

Restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis has been ordered and will be completed within the next 7-10 days; results will be reviewed at a follow-up appointment. Today's visit focuses on clinical assessment and transition planning.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
- Constitutional: Mild fatigue (Grade 1), no fevers, no significant unintentional weight loss
- Pulmonary: No cough, no dyspnea at rest, occasional mild exertional dyspnea (unchanged from baseline post-nephrectomy, likely cardiac/deconditioning)
- GI: Resolved colitis — currently no diarrhea (1 formed stool per day, at baseline), no abdominal cramping, no nausea, no blood in stool, appetite intact
- MSK: No arthralgias, myalgias, or joint swelling
- Neurologic: No headache, focal deficits, or cognitive changes
- Skin: No rash, pruritus, or mucositis
- Endocrine: Denies polyuria, polydipsia; mild cold intolerance and constipation noted over the past 3-4 weeks (see below)
- Hematologic: No easy bruising or bleeding
- Renal/GU: Adapted well to single kidney; no dysuria or hematuria
- Cardiovascular: No chest pain, palpitations, or leg swelling

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- Stage IV ccRCC, right kidney, with sarcomatoid differentiation (20%), bilateral pulmonary metastases, and retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy — as above
- Hypertension — chronic, managed with amlodipine
- Remote 20 pack-year smoking history (quit 15 years prior)
- Right radical nephrectomy with RPLND (cytoreductive intent, ~4 months ago)

PAST SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Right radical nephrectomy with retroperitoneal lymph node dissection (open approach, cytoreductive, R0)

FAMILY HISTORY:
- No family history of renal cell carcinoma or hereditary cancer syndromes per prior documentation. Patient declined germline evaluation for BAP1 at this time.

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Retired schoolteacher
- Remote smoker (quit 15 years ago, 20 pack-year history)
- Non-drinker (social alcohol use, rare)
- Lives with spouse; good family support system
- Functionally independent at home

ALLERGIES:
- NKDA

MEDICATIONS (current):
1. Amlodipine 5 mg orally daily (hypertension)
2. Prednisone taper — completed (last dose within the past week per patient report)
3. Omeprazole 20 mg orally daily (GI prophylaxis during steroid taper — to discontinue)

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vital Signs: BP 128/82 mmHg, HR 76 bpm, RR 16, Temp 98.4°F, SpO2 98% on room air, Weight 72 kg (stable from last visit)
ECOG Performance Status: 1

General: Alert and oriented x3, well-nourished woman in no acute distress. Conversant and engaged.
HEENT: Sclerae anicteric, no oral mucositis, no lymphadenopathy in cervical, supraclavicular, or axillary chains.
Chest/Pulmonary: Clear to auscultation bilaterally without wheezes, rhonchi, or rales. No pleural friction rub.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs, gallops, or rubs. No lower extremity edema.
Abdomen: Well-healed right flank incision without erythema or dehiscence. Abdomen soft, non-tender, non-distended. No palpable masses or hepatosplenomegaly. Active bowel sounds.
Skin: No rash, no palmar-plantar erythema, no petechiae or ecchymoses.
Musculoskeletal: No joint swelling, tenderness, or effusion.
Neurologic: Grossly intact; no focal deficits.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS (today):
- CBC: WBC 7.2 K/uL (normal differential), Hgb 11.1 g/dL (mild anemia, stable), Plt 198 K/uL
- CMP: Na 138, K 4.1, Cr 1.3 mg/dL (stable post-nephrectomy baseline), BUN 18, eGFR ~52 mL/min/1.73m2, ALT 22 U/L, AST 19 U/L, Alk Phos 78 U/L, Total bili 0.6 mg/dL, albumin 3.8 g/dL
- Glucose: 98 mg/dL (note: was on steroids — glucose now normalizing off steroids)
- TSH: 6.8 mIU/L (elevated; baseline was 2.1 mIU/L pre-treatment) — flagged
- Free T4: 0.7 ng/dL (low; reference 0.8-1.8) — flagged
- LDH: 198 U/L (within normal limits)
- UA: unremarkable

---

IMAGING:
Restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast ordered today — appointment scheduled for this week. Results pending at time of this note. Prior CT at diagnosis demonstrated multiple bilateral pulmonary nodules (dominant right lower lobe lesion 1.8 cm), no osseous metastases, and retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy (addressed surgically). This will be the first formal post-treatment restaging evaluation.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

1. STAGE IV CLEAR CELL RCC — TRANSITIONING FROM INDUCTION TO MAINTENANCE IMMUNOTHERAPY
Ms. Okonkwo has completed all 4 planned induction cycles of ipilimumab + nivolumab (Checkmate 214 regimen), with the course complicated by Grade 2 immune colitis requiring ipi hold x1 cycle and steroid treatment (now fully tapered and resolved). Per planned protocol, she will now transition to nivolumab (Opdivo) 480 mg flat-dose IV every 4 weeks as maintenance monotherapy. This was discussed in detail today, including the rationale (durable disease control, ongoing IO exposure without additional ipi-related toxicity risk), expected side effect profile, and continued immune monitoring requirements.
- Plan: Initiate nivolumab 480 mg IV q4 weeks starting today (first maintenance dose).
- Restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis to be performed within the next 7-10 days; results to be reviewed at next scheduled visit.
- Patient verbalized understanding and agreement with treatment plan.

2. IMMUNE-RELATED COLITIS — RESOLVED
Grade 2 irAE colitis, now resolved. Steroids fully tapered and completed. Infectious workup negative. Bowel function returned to baseline. No colonoscopy required given clinical Grade 2 presentation and complete response to steroids.
- Omeprazole to be discontinued now that steroid taper is complete.
- Monitor for recurrence of GI symptoms with restart of immunotherapy.
- If diarrhea recurs, will evaluate promptly and consider early scope given prior colitis history.

3. IMMUNE-RELATED HYPOTHYROIDISM — NEW FINDING
Laboratory findings today are consistent with Grade 2 immune-mediated hypothyroidism: TSH 6.8 mIU/L (elevated) and free T4 0.7 ng/dL (low). Clinically, patient endorses mild cold intolerance and constipation over the past several weeks, and the fatigue she notes likely has a thyroid contribution in addition to treatment-related causes. This pattern is consistent with checkpoint inhibitor-induced thyroiditis/hypothyroidism, a common irAE with PD-1 agents.
- Levothyroxine 75 mcg orally daily initiated today.
- TFTs to be rechecked in 6-8 weeks.
- No steroid therapy required for isolated hypothyroidism.
- No nivolumab dose modification warranted per NCCN guidelines for Grade 2 endocrine irAE.
- Patient counseled on symptoms of under- and over-replacement; advised to take levothyroxine on an empty stomach in the morning.

4. HYPERTENSION
- Stable on amlodipine 5 mg daily; BP 128/82 today, well-controlled.
- Continue current regimen.

5. ANEMIA (MILD, CHRONIC)
- Hgb 11.1 g/dL, stable from prior checks. Likely multifactorial (chronic disease, prior post-surgical anemia, single-kidney CKD stage 3a). No acute blood loss. Iron studies and B12/folate to be checked at next visit if persists.
- No intervention required at this time.

6. RENAL FUNCTION / SINGLE KIDNEY STATUS
- Creatinine 1.3 mg/dL (eGFR ~52 mL/min/1.73m2), stable since nephrectomy. Consistent with expected CKD3a on solitary kidney.
- Continue to monitor; avoid nephrotoxic agents. Ensure adequate hydration.

7. HEALTH MAINTENANCE / SUPPORTIVE CARE
- Patient referred for dietary counseling given mild weight loss.
- Encouraged light physical activity as tolerated.
- Next scheduled visit: follow-up in 3-4 weeks for restaging CT review and toxicity assessment on nivolumab maintenance.
- Social work referral to be placed given long-term treatment trajectory and patient's expressed psychosocial concerns.

---

RETURN TO CLINIC: 3-4 weeks (or sooner PRN for new symptoms, particularly GI symptoms or shortness of breath).

Time spent: 45 minutes, more than 50% of which was counseling and coordination of care.

[Electronically signed — Attending Medical Oncologist]
